note-extraction.ceiling_note: output-exists criterion (10% weight) always scores 0 due to testPattern 'entities\.json exists' not matching JUnit testcase name 'entities.json file is present'

medication-reconciliation.ceiling_note: Three criteria (output-exists 10%, admission-count 15%, discharge-count 15%) always score 0 due to testPattern mismatch: describe block names don't appear in JUnit testcase names (e.g., pattern 'admission list has 5' doesn't match testcase 'admission list has exactly 5 medications')

changes_made.description: Compressed SKILL.md from 270 lines to ~50 lines. Removed verbose Python code examples, ICD-10 codes, Top 20 drug list, and redundant pattern documentation. Added direct instruction to construct JSON manually instead of writing complex Python scripts. Focused on exact output format specifications for both tasks.
changes_made.impact: medication-reconciliation went from timeout to 60/100; note-extraction completion time dropped from 5m to 1m

notes: Both tasks achieve their maximum possible scores given systematic test evaluation ceiling. The eval framework parses JUnit XML testcase names but the task criteria testPatterns reference describe block names, causing 3 criteria in medication-reconciliation and 1 in note-extraction to always return rawScore=0 regardless of actual test pass/fail status.
